dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8042 IH: Empowering Women in Agriculture Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Cherfilus-McCormick (for herself, Mr. Jackson of Illinois, Ms. Salinas, and Ms. Brownley) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 to expand outreach and assistance to socially disadvantaged farmers and ranchers who are women, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Empowering Women in Agriculture Act.

subsection.text: Section 2501(a) of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 2279(a)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (6), by striking racial or ethnic prejudice and inserting racial, gender, or ethnic prejudice; and
by adding at the end the following:
The term women’s nonprofit organization means an organization that—

subparagraph.text: was founded by, and for the purpose of serving, women;
is an organization described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of such Code; and
has been in continuous existence for at least 10 years.

subsection.header: Outreach and assistance for socially disadvantaged and veteran farmers and ranchers
subsection.text: Section 2501(c)(1) of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 2279(c)(1)) is amended by adding at the end the following:
Section 2501(l) of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 (7 U.S.C. 2279(l)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2), by striking fiscal years 2019 through 2023 and inserting fiscal years 2021 through 2031; and
in paragraph (4), by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.header: Socially disadvantaged women farmers and ranchers
subparagraph.text: Not less that 10 percent of the amounts made available to carry out subsection (c) for a fiscal year shall be used to support programs and services that address the needs of women who are socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers.
